speaker: interviewer
text: And I don't have words to express. I don't know what to say. It's unbelievable what we-- they doing to my-- to me and to my family-- unbelievable. I don't have words. I don't know what to tell.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK.

speaker: interviewee
text: OK.

speaker: interviewer
text: And Laura, now, we're going to see your grandchildren. You'll show us some pictures.

speaker: interviewee
text: OK. I also want to add one more thing, if I may.

speaker: interviewer
text: Sure.

speaker: interviewee
text: Lately, I hear a lot of people referring to the Holocaust as other events that are happening. They compare it to the Holocaust. They say, this is a Holocaust. This is the Holocaust. And I am very resentful when I hear that, because I think people using that word too loosely-- because if they would really understand what happened during the Holocaust, they would not compare it. There's nothing to compare it to. They are-- when they are speaking of tragic things that happen-- that are happening-- which I don't disagree-- but they're not holocausts. They cannot compare it to the Holocaust. There were no gas chambers. They didn't throw live children into the-- in-- into fires and things like that. So that really annoys me when I hear so many people using that word very loosely.

speaker: interviewer
text: All right. Well, thank you both very much for a wonderful interview.

speaker: interviewer
text: I think you're supposed to derive of my [NON-ENGLISH]. And remember, it's because he-- he was an angel, because he were-- made a God, as angel. And if-- if he was catch out, he was killed, him, and his wife, and the children. And they burn him out-- the whole thing. And he didn't even took finance-- not even one penny.

text: The people, when they took the-- my-- my-- my brother, Michael-- 20 years old. He was already engaged. He--

And then later the night, when he came up, and then after the-- they said, no more Jews-- be killed, I see he's coming up with a new crate with my brother. It was a [NON-ENGLISH]. Probably he was keeping the-- the-- he killed him, rather. And he had his clothing. Yeah.
